evals.prompt: Scout Jane Zhu from Redmond, WA.
evals.expected_output: A .docx report in reports/ for the Redmond, WA Jane Zhu. Header shows DR and NTRP, report includes TL;DR, volume split (S vs D), overall and split W-L, opponent difficulty stats (mean/median/min/max), rating trend label with slope, top wins and upset losses, court + partner breakdowns, and a full match log covering the last 24 months.
pass_criteria: Output file exists under reports/ with a date-stamped filename
Title block shows NTRP, rating type (C or S), and DR with an 'as of' date
TL;DR contains 3–5 bullet points
Opponent strength section shows split record vs tougher / peer / easier opponents
Match log row count equals the number of matches in the 24-month window

evals.expected_output: Report includes a prominent self-rated warning near the top in dark red bold. Analysis treats the player's listed NTRP as potentially under-representing their true level.
pass_criteria: Rating type in title block is 'S'
Self-rated warning paragraph appears before the TL;DR, bold and dark red
TL;DR mentions the self-rated status explicitly

evals.prompt: Scout John Smith — no location given.
evals.expected_output: Direct profile fetch without `s` resolves ambiguously or to the wrong player. Skill asks the user for the exact TennisRecord profile URL or `&s=` disambiguator instead of trying a POST search.
pass_criteria: Skill does not use the TennisRecord POST search form
Skill does not auto-pick a candidate
User is asked for the exact profile URL or `s` value
No .docx is written before disambiguation

evals.prompt: Scout a player whose last 24 months contain only 3 matches.
evals.expected_output: Report generates successfully with the small sample but includes a caveat that the sample size is too small for a stable rating-trend signal.
pass_criteria: Report generates despite a 3-match window
Rating-trend section includes a 'small sample' caveat
Notable-matches section shows at most 3 entries per category (wins / upsets) without error
Slope label is reported but not used as a strong claim

evals.prompt: Scout a player whose recent match history shows a sudden +0.4 rating jump between consecutive matches.
evals.expected_output: Report flags the rating jump in the TL;DR as a possible self-rated bump, independent of the C/S label.
pass_criteria: TL;DR includes a bullet calling out the rating jump
Rating Trend section shows the specific match where the jump occurred
Flag appears whether the player is labeled C or S

evals.prompt: Scout 'Xyz Nonexistent' — a name that returns zero search results.
evals.expected_output: Skill reports no matches, suggests checking spelling or providing a location, and does not write a report.
pass_criteria: No .docx is written to reports/
User-facing message says no matches were found and suggests spelling / location hints
Skill does not hang or retry indefinitely

evals.expected_output: usta-player-scout should NOT trigger — this is a team-level request. The usta-team-scout skill should handle it instead.
pass_criteria: usta-player-scout is not activated
The request is routed to usta-team-scout (or the user is redirected there)

evals.prompt: Scout a player whose profile page returns HTTP 429 mid-fetch.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects the 429 from fetch_webpage, stops, surfaces a clear message, and does not write a partial report.
pass_criteria: No .docx file is written
Message to user explains the rate limit and suggests trying again later
No aggressive retry loop — at most one polite retry with backoff
